text.title: Internal Link Rotation Schedule Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every internal-link rotation schedule must enforce. Hard rules; designed to prevent schedule-slip, SLA-erosion, audit-theatre, and missing retrospectives.

rule.statement: Exactly 1 cluster is audited per fortnight (every 2 weeks). For 3-10 clusters, rotation covers the site in 6-20 weeks.
rule.rationale: More frequent than fortnightly burns operator time; less frequent than monthly lets issues compound between audits.

rule.statement: Audit window is fixed at 60 minutes. Overruns are deferred (logged into the deferred-fix register), not absorbed by extending the slot.
rule.rationale: Time-boxed audits sustain over quarters; unbounded audits get cancelled by the third rotation.

rule.statement: Every deferred fix carries a 30-day SLA from audit date. Fixes past SLA escalate into the next rotation's first slot.
rule.rationale: Without an SLA, deferred-fix lists become graveyards; the 30-day cap forces resolution before stale-rot.

rule.statement: Cluster rotation order is fixed at quarter start. Reorderings mid-quarter are forbidden — favouring one cluster creates blind spots elsewhere.
rule.rationale: Operators tend to repeatedly audit the "interesting" cluster and skip the boring ones; fixed order breaks the bias.

rule.statement: No rotation slot is skipped. If the operator cannot run a slot, the schedule pauses (with reason logged) rather than silently rolling forward.
rule.rationale: Silent skips become the new norm; explicit pause makes the gap visible and recoverable.

rule.statement: End of quarter: write a 1-page retrospective summarising fixes shipped, SLA misses, recurring offender clusters, and rotation-order adjustments for the next quarter.
rule.rationale: Without retrospective, the schedule loops without learning. The retrospective is the only mechanism that improves cluster-specific rules over time.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the 5-step procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring the schedule live.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree run leaf for canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree skip leaf for non-applicable inputs.
